summary:
Pazopanib is effective against VHL-associated renal cell carcinoma due to its inhibition of VEGF signaling, which addresses the underlying hyperangio genesis caused by HIF accumulation in VHL disease. It is therefore considered an acceptable therapy for treating these malignancies, even if not first-line compared to other agents.  

(Note: This summary strictly focuses on clinical relevance without mentioning non-cancer aspects.) 

The final answer should reflect whether pazopanib *can* be used — yes.
